title: Fix: Customer form submissions not appearing in admin dashboard immediately after submission

requirements.text: After a customer successfully submits a service order form, the admin dashboard must immediately reflect the new order without requiring a manual page refresh. The admin order list should be invalidated and refetched automatically upon any new order submission.

quotes: Jab customer Abhi Abhi turant hi Apna form fil Kiya lekin abhi admin ke dust board Mein show nahin kar raha hai form

acceptanceCriteria: When a customer submits a service form, the new order appears in the admin dashboard without any manual refresh.
The admin dashboard order list is automatically re-fetched/invalidated after a new order is created.
No stale cache prevents the new order from being visible to the admin immediately after submission.

requirements.text: The admin dashboard must poll or auto-refresh the orders list at a regular interval (e.g., every 10–15 seconds) so that any new customer submissions are visible in near real-time, even when the admin dashboard is already open.

quotes: abhi admin ke dust board Mein show nahin kar raha hai form

acceptanceCriteria: The admin dashboard periodically refetches the orders list automatically while the admin is on the dashboard screen.
New orders submitted by customers appear in the admin view within the polling interval without any manual action.

requirements.text: Verify that the backend `getOrders` (or equivalent admin order-fetching) endpoint correctly returns all submitted orders sorted by most recent first, including orders that were just submitted moments ago. Ensure there are no in-memory or stable storage inconsistencies that could cause a freshly submitted order to be temporarily invisible.

quotes: Jab customer Abhi Abhi turant hi Apna form fil Kiya lekin abhi admin ke dust board Mein show nahin kar raha hai form

acceptanceCriteria: Immediately after a `submitOrder` call succeeds, a subsequent `getOrders` call returns the new order.
Orders are returned sorted with the newest order first.
No race condition or buffering in the backend causes a submitted order to be omitted from the list.

constraints: Do not change the customer-facing service form submission flow or its success screen.
Do not alter authentication logic in AuthContext or useInternetIdentity hooks.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor file (backend/main.mo).
nonGoals: Real-time WebSocket or push notification support.
Changes to the QR code management feature.
Any modifications to the customer login or registration flow.
